goal: 修复 context --files 多路径参数解析

summary.title: context --files accepts trailing path arguments
summary.what: Updated the context CLI request builder so files mode appends remaining positional arguments to the --files values, while current/query/legacy modes reject unexpected positional arguments. Added focused CLI parsing regression tests for trailing files, repeated/comma-parsed files, mode exclusivity, and legacy no-mode behavior.
summary.why: The command advertises --files <path>... and context has no positional operands of its own, so a natural multi-file call should include every path instead of silently querying only the first one.
summary.user_goal: 修复 context --files 多路径参数解析，避免静默吞掉第二个文件

decisions.chose: Keep the existing flag-based context interface instead of introducing context subcommands.
decisions.rationale: Other Mainline commands already use flags for modes and filters; preserving that style fixes the bug without fragmenting the CLI.

decisions.chose: Append leftover positional args to the pflag-parsed --files slice only when files mode is active.
decisions.rationale: pflag consumes the first --files value; because context has no positional operands, the remaining args are unambiguous additional file paths.

decisions.chose: Reject extra positional args for --current, --query, and bare legacy context.
decisions.rationale: Failing loudly avoids the same class of silent agent misreads outside files mode.

rejected.reason: That would solve varargs but would also make context unlike the rest of the flag-oriented CLI surface.
The help already implies multi-path input and the command can safely support it.

risks: Files mode now treats any leftover positional argument as a path. This is intentional for context because there are no other positional operands, but future context positional syntax must revisit this parser boundary.
The legacy bare context state dump remains for compatibility; only bare context with stray positional arguments now fails.

anti_patterns.what: Silently ignoring positional arguments passed to context retrieval modes
anti_patterns.why: Agents may believe all requested files were included in intent retrieval and make decisions from incomplete context.

architectural_claims: context remains a flag-oriented retrieval command; files mode owns leftover positional args as file paths
behavioral_changes: mainline context --files a b --json now queries both a and b instead of silently ignoring b
mainline context --query text extra and mainline context --current extra now return INVALID_INPUT
migration_notes: Existing --files a,b and repeated --files forms continue to work
